content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 17,
1992
Jean Song
Friends of Xavier Becerra
112 5.
Taylor Avenue
Montebello,
CA
90640
RE:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A-92—596
Dear Ms.
Song:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.
(Government Code Sections 81000—91015.)
Your letter of October 22,
1992,
correctly summarizes my
advice that the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was not
required to file the 2nd pre-election campaign statement due
October 22nd in connection with the November 3,
1992 election.
Assemblyman Becerra is running for U.S.
Congress and made a
contribution out of his personal funds to support another
congressional candidate.
The Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee
had not received any contributions or made any expenditures during
the period of October
1 through October 17,
1992.
The committee
will be required to file its next campaign statement by January
31,
1993,
covering the period from the day after the closing date
of the last campaign statement filed through December 31,
1992.
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at 916/322—5661.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Political Reform Consultant
428 j Street, Suite 800
•
RU. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was required to file a second pre-election campaign statement for the November 3, 1992 election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was not required to file the second pre-election campaign statement due October 22, 1992, because it had received no contributions and made no expenditures during October 1-17, 1992. The committee's next required filing is a campaign statement due January 31, 1993, covering October 18, 1992 through December 31, 1992.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of prior telephone advice rather than an initial response to a written request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; information extracted from letter body. Strong confidence in extraction of key facts and legal conclusion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was required to file a second pre-election campaign statement for the November 3, 1992 election.

CONCLUSION: The Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was not required to file the second pre-election campaign statement due October 22, 1992, because it had received no contributions and made no expenditures during October 1-17, 1992. The committee's next required filing is a campaign statement due January 31, 1993, covering October 18, 1992 through December 31, 1992.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission November 17, 1992 Jean Song Friends of Xavier Becerra 112 5. Taylor Avenue Montebello, CA 90640 RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. A-92—596 Dear Ms. Song: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on behalf of the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000—91015.) Your letter of October 22, 1992, correctly summarizes my advice that the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee was not required to file the 2nd pre-election campaign statement due October 22nd in connection with the November 3, 1992 election. Assemblyman Becerra is running for U.S. Congress and made a contribution out of his personal funds to support another congressional candidate. The Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee had not received any contributions or made any expenditures during the period of October 1 through October 17, 1992. The committee will be required to file its next campaign statement by January 31, 1993, covering the period from the day after the closing date of the last campaign statement filed through December 31, 1992. If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at 916/322—5661. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel Political Reform Consultant 428 j Street, Suite 800 • RU. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation letter to the Friends of Xavier Becerra Committee advising that no second pre-election campaign statement was required for the November 3, 1992 election due to lack of contributions or expenditures, with next filing deadline of January 31, 1993.
